docs.title: mydborm
docs.text:
mydborm is a production-grade lightweight Python ORM for MySQL 8+ and YugabyteDB (YSQL).

Zero bloat. Declarative models. Full CRUD. Bulk ops. Async. Migrations. CLI included.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install mydborm
pip install mydborm[cli]    # CLI support
pip install mydborm[async]  # Async support

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
from mydborm import db, BaseModel, IntField, StrField, BoolField

db.configure(
    dialect  = "mysql",
    host     = "127.0.0.1",
    port     = 3306,
    user     = "root",
    password = "yourpassword",
    database = "mydb",
)

class User(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    id       = IntField(primary_key=True)
    username = StrField(max_length=100, nullable=False)
    active   = BoolField(default=True)

User.create_table()

uid  = User.create(username="alice", active=True)
user = User.get(id=uid)
print(user.username)  # alice

users = User.query().where("active", True).order_by("username").all()

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature Status Declarative models with 11 field types ✅ Full CRUD — create, get, all, filter, update, delete ✅ QueryBuilder — where, join, group_by, having, subquery ✅ Relationships — has_many, belongs_to, many_to_many ✅ Lazy + eager loading ✅ Session — identity map, change tracking, unit of work ✅ Bulk operations with chunking + retry ✅ Transactions + savepoints ✅ Schema migrations + auto-generation ✅ Custom validators ✅ Async support — aiomysql + aiopg ✅ Connection pooling ✅ MySQL + YugabyteDB dialect support ✅ UTF-8 / unicode support ✅ Rich CLI — 7 commands ✅ 561 tests — 88% coverage ✅

docs.title: mydborm vs SQLAlchemy vs Peewee (MySQL)
docs.text: Operation mydborm SQLAlchemy Peewee Winner Single insert x1000 11.0s 4.1s 7.8s SQLAlchemy Bulk insert x5000 0.24s 0.29s 0.33s mydborm Select all x1000 0.032s 0.010s 0.119s SQLAlchemy Filter x1000 0.023s 0.007s 0.103s SQLAlchemy Update x1000 0.011s 0.013s 0.007s Peewee

docs.title: mydborm: MySQL vs YugabyteDB
docs.text: Operation MySQL YugabyteDB Faster Single insert x1000 11.0s 8.4s YugabyteDB Bulk insert x5000 0.24s 0.45s MySQL Select all x1000 0.032s 0.060s MySQL Update x1000 0.011s 0.219s MySQL

docs.title: Key takeaway
docs.text: Use bulk_create() for large datasets — mydborm is the fastest at bulk inserts. For single-row inserts, wrap in db.transaction() for a 3x speedup.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• MkDocs documentation site
  • Coverage 95%+
  • Full type hints
  • API stability guarantee

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Auto-migration generation — generate(), apply_migration_file()
  • GROUP BY + HAVING in QueryBuilder
  • Subquery support
  • Performance benchmarks

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Coverage 44% → 88%
  • Custom validators — EmailValidator, RangeValidator, etc.
  • test_dialects, test_migrations, test_cli

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Session — identity map, change tracking, unit of work
  • Lazy loading via LazyRelation descriptor
  • Eager loading via QueryBuilder.include()

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• 24 custom exception types
  • Chunked bulk operations with BulkResult + retry
  • Savepoints + nested transactions
  • bulk_upsert — ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
  • JOIN support — inner, left, right
  • Model serialization — to_dict, to_json, from_dict
  • Schema validation

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • YugabyteDB dialect — SERIAL, BOOLEAN, JSONB, RETURNING id

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Bulk operations — bulk_create, bulk_update, bulk_delete
  • Raw SQL — db.execute, fetchall, fetchone
  • Async ORM — AsyncConnectionManager, AsyncBaseModel
  • Connection pooling — configure_pool, ping, reconnect

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• QueryBuilder with operators, order, limit, offset
  • Aggregates — sum, avg, min, max, count
  • Relationships — has_many, belongs_to, many_to_many
  • GitHub Actions CI

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Core ORM — BaseModel, 11 field types
  • Schema migrations
  • Rich CLI — version, ping, tables, inspect, migrate

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Initial release

docs.title: mydborm.db.ConnectionManager
docs.text:
Central connection manager.

Usage — direct config: db.configure(dialect="mysql", host="localhost", user="root", password="root", database="testdb")

Usage — from environment variable: os.environ["DATABASE_URL"] = "mysql://root:root@localhost:3306/testdb" db.from_env()

Usage — as context manager: with db.connect() as conn: cur = conn.cursor() cur.execute("SELECT 1")

docs.title: encoding property
docs.text: Python encoding for text handling (default utf-8).

docs.title: bulk_transaction()
docs.text:
Atomic transaction across multiple model operations. ALL operations commit together or ALL roll back together.

Usage

with db.bulk_transaction() as tx: tx.execute("INSERT INTO users ...") tx.execute("INSERT INTO profiles ...") tx.execute("INSERT INTO orders ...")

The tx object is the connection — use db.execute() inside.

docs.title: close()
docs.text: Close the current thread's connection.

docs.title: configure(**kwargs)
docs.text:
Set connection config directly as keyword arguments.

Other Parameters:

Name Type Description dialect str

"mysql" or "yugabyte"

host str

database host

port int

database port

user str

database user

password str

database password

database str

database name

charset str

character set (default utf8mb4 for MySQL)

encoding str

python encoding (default utf-8)

docs.title: configure_pool(pool_size=5, max_overflow=10, pool_timeout=30, pool_recycle=3600)
docs.text:
Configure connection pool settings.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default pool_size int

number of persistent connections (default 5)

5 max_overflow int

extra connections allowed above pool_size

10 pool_timeout int

seconds to wait for a connection (default 30)

30 pool_recycle int

seconds before recycling a connection (default 3600)

3600 Usage

db.configure(dialect="mysql", ...) db.configure_pool(pool_size=10, max_overflow=20)

docs.title: connect()
docs.text:
Thread-safe connection with automatic commit / rollback.

with db.connect() as conn: cur = conn.cursor() cur.execute("INSERT INTO ...")

docs.title: execute(sql, params=None)
docs.text:
Execute a raw SQL statement (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL). Returns number of affected rows.

Usage

db.execute("UPDATE users SET active = %s WHERE id = %s", [False, 1])

docs.title: fetchall(sql, params=None)
docs.text:
Execute a raw SELECT and return list of dicts.

Usage

rows = db.fetchall( "SELECT * FROM users WHERE active = %s", [True] )

docs.title: fetchone(sql, params=None)
docs.text:
Execute a raw SELECT and return a single row dict or None.

Usage

row = db.fetchone( "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s", ["alice@example.com"] )

docs.title: from_env(var='DATABASE_URL')
docs.text: Load config from an environment variable. Works on Windows, Linux, and macOS.

docs.title: list_tables()
docs.text:
Return list of all table names in the current database.

Usage

tables = db.list_tables() print(tables) # ['users', 'products', ...]

docs.title: nested_transaction()
docs.text:
Create a nested transaction using savepoints. If already inside a transaction, uses a savepoint. If not, starts a new transaction.

Usage

with db.transaction(): User.create(username="alice") with db.nested_transaction(): User.create(username="bob") # if this fails, only bob rolls back

docs.title: ping()
docs.text:
Ping the database to check connectivity. Returns True if connected, False otherwise.

Usage

if db.ping(): print("Database is reachable")

docs.title: pool_status()
docs.text:
Return current pool configuration and connection status.

Usage

status = db.pool_status() print(status)

docs.title: reconnect()
docs.text:
Force close and reopen the connection. Useful after database restarts or stale connections.

Usage

db.reconnect()

docs.title: savepoint(name=None)
docs.text:
Create a savepoint within an active transaction. Allows partial rollback without rolling back the entire transaction.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

savepoint name (auto-generated if not provided)

None Usage

with db.transaction(): User.create(username="alice") with db.savepoint("after_alice"): User.create(username="bob") raise Exception("bob failed") # only bob is rolled back, alice is kept

docs.title: table_exists(table)
docs.text:
Check if a table exists in the current database.

Usage

if db.table_exists("users"): print("Table exists")

docs.title: transaction()
docs.text:
Explicit transaction context manager. All statements inside the block are committed together. Any exception triggers a full rollback.

Usage

with db.transaction() as conn: db.execute("INSERT INTO users ...") db.execute("INSERT INTO profiles ...")

docs.title: both committed or both rolled back
transaction_with_retry(retries=3, retry_delay=0.5)
docs.text: Transaction that retries on deadlock with exponential backoff. Non-deadlock exceptions are raised immediately without retry.

docs.title: mydborm.exceptions.MydbormError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base exception for all mydborm errors. Catch this to handle any mydborm-related error.

Usage

try: User.create(username="alice") except MydbormError as e: print(f"ORM error: {e}")

docs.title: mydborm.exceptions.BulkInsertError
docs.text:
Bases: BulkOperationError

Raised when bulk_create fails on one or more chunks.

docs.title: mydborm.exceptions.ValidationError
docs.text:
Bases: MydbormError

Raised when field validation fails before a DB operation.

Attributes:

Name Type Description field

the field name that failed

value

the value that was rejected

reason

human-readable reason

docs.title: mydborm.exceptions.SchemaError
docs.text:
Bases: MydbormError

Raised when model schema doesn't match the live database.

Attributes:

Name Type Description table

table name with the mismatch

missing_columns

columns in model but not in DB

extra_columns

columns in DB but not in model

docs.title: mydborm.fields.Field
docs.text: Base class for all field types. Every field maps to a database column.

docs.title: to_sql_def(dialect='mysql')
docs.text: Return the SQL column definition string.

docs.title: validate(value)
docs.text: Validate and coerce value before insert/update.

docs.title: mydborm.fields.IntField
docs.text:
Bases: Field

Integer column — INT in MySQL, INTEGER in YSQL.

docs.title: mydborm.fields.StrField
docs.text:
Bases: Field

Variable-length string — VARCHAR(n).

docs.title: mydborm.fields.BoolField
docs.text:
Bases: Field

Boolean — TINYINT(1) in MySQL, BOOLEAN in PostgreSQL/Yugabyte.

docs.title: mydborm.fields.FloatField

Bases: Field

Floating point — FLOAT column.

docs.title: mydborm.fields.EmailValidator
docs.text:
Bases: ValidationRule

Validates email address format.

docs.title: mydborm.fields.RangeValidator
docs.text:
Bases: ValidationRule

Validates numeric value is within a range.

docs.title: mydborm.migrations.migrate(model_class, description='')
docs.text:
Apply migrations for a model class.

Returns a result dict: { "table" : "users", "sqls" : [...], "applied" : True | False, "message" : "...", }

docs.title: mydborm.migrations.generate(model_class, output_dir='migrations', description='', apply=False)
docs.text:
Generate a versioned SQL migration file from model diff.

Compares model definition against live DB schema and writes a numbered SQL file to output_dir.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model_class

BaseModel subclass to generate migration for

required output_dir str

directory to write migration files

'migrations' description str

optional description for the migration

'' apply bool

if True, also apply the migration immediately

False

Returns:

Type Description dict

{ "file" : path to generated file (or None if up to date), "version" : migration version string, "sqls" : list of SQL statements, "applied" : True if migration was applied, "message" : status message,

dict

}

Usage

from mydborm.migrations import generate

result = generate(User, output_dir="migrations/") print(result["file"])

docs.title: mydborm.migrations.migration_status()
docs.text:
Return all applied migrations from the tracking table.

Returns list of dicts with keys

id, version, description, checksum, applied_at, rolled_back

docs.title: mydborm.migrations.rollback(model_class)
docs.text:
Roll back the last migration for a model by dropping its table. Marks the migration as rolled_back in the tracking table.

Use with caution — this drops the table and all its data.

docs.title: mydborm.model.BaseModel
docs.text:
Inherit from this to define a database model.

Example

class User(BaseModel): tablename = "users" id = IntField(primary_key=True) username = StrField(max_length=100, nullable=False) email = StrField(max_length=255, nullable=False, unique=True) active = BoolField(default=True)

docs.title: Create table
docs.text: User.create_table()

docs.title: Insert
docs.text: User.create(username="alice", email="alice@example.com")

docs.title: Query
docs.text: users = User.all() user = User.get(id=1) devs = User.filter(active=True)

docs.title: Update
docs.text: User.update({"active": False}, id=1)

docs.title: Delete
docs.text: User.delete(id=1)

docs.title: all() classmethod
docs.text: Return all rows as list of dicts.

docs.title: belongs_to(related_model, foreign_key=None)
docs.text:
Return the parent record this instance belongs to.

Usage

book = Book.get(id=1) author = book.belongs_to(Author, foreign_key="author_id")

If foreign_key is omitted, defaults to: _id e.g. "author_id"

docs.title: bulk_create(records) classmethod
docs.text:
Insert multiple rows in a single query. Returns number of inserted rows.

Usage

User.bulk_create([ {"username": "alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}, {"username": "bob", "email": "bob@example.com"}, ])

docs.title: bulk_delete(ids, key='id') classmethod
docs.text:
Delete multiple rows by a list of key values. Returns number of deleted rows.

Usage

User.bulk_delete([1, 2, 3]) User.bulk_delete(["alice", "bob"], key="username")

docs.title: bulk_update(records, key='id') classmethod
docs.text:
Update multiple rows. Each record must contain the key field. Returns total number of affected rows.

Usage

User.bulk_update([ {"id": 1, "active": False}, {"id": 2, "active": False}, ])

docs.title: bulk_upsert(records, conflict_key='id', update_fields=None, create_index=True) classmethod
docs.text:
Insert records or update on conflict — dialect aware.

MySQL → INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE YugabyteDB → INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default records list

list of dicts to insert/update

required conflict_key str

unique field that determines conflict

'id' update_fields list

fields to update on conflict

None create_index bool

auto-create UNIQUE index on conflict_keys

True

Returns:

Type Description int

number of affected rows

docs.title: count(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text: Count rows, optionally filtered.

docs.title: create(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text:
Insert a new row. Returns the new row's primary key.

User.create(username="alice", email="alice@example.com")

docs.title: create_table(if_not_exists=True) classmethod
docs.text: Create the database table for this model.

docs.title: delete(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text:
Delete rows matching kwargs. Returns number of deleted rows.

User.delete(id=1)

docs.title: drop_table(if_exists=True) classmethod
docs.text: Drop the database table for this model.

docs.title: exists(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text: Return True if any row matches kwargs.

docs.title: filter(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text:
Return all rows matching kwargs.

User.filter(active=True)

docs.title: from_dict(data) classmethod
docs.text:
Create a ModelInstance from a plain dict WITHOUT saving to DB.

Usage

user = User.from_dict({"id": 1, "username": "alice"}) print(user.username)

docs.title: from_json(json_str) classmethod
docs.text:
Create a ModelInstance from a JSON string WITHOUT saving to DB.

Usage

user = User.from_json('{"id": 1, "username": "alice"}')

docs.title: get(**kwargs) classmethod
docs.text:
Return a single row matching kwargs or None.

User.get(id=1)

docs.title: has_many(related_model, foreign_key=None)
docs.text:
Return all related records where foreign_key = self.pk.

Usage

author = Author.get(id=1) books = author.has_many(Book, foreign_key="author_id")

If foreign_key is omitted, defaults to: _id e.g. "author_id"

docs.title: many_to_many(related_model, join_table, source_key=None, target_key=None)
docs.text:
Return all related records via a join table.

Usage

student = Student.get(id=1) courses = student.many_to_many( Course, join_table="student_courses", source_key="student_id", target_key="course_id" )

If source_key / target_key are omitted they default to: _id and _id

docs.title: query() classmethod
docs.text:
Return a QueryBuilder for this model.

Usage

User.query().where("active", True).order_by("name").all()

docs.title: schema_info() classmethod
docs.text:
Return model schema information — fields, types, constraints.

Usage

info = User.schema_info() for field, details in info["fields"].items(): print(field, details)

docs.title: update(data, **where_kwargs) classmethod
docs.text:
Update rows matching where_kwargs with data. Returns number of affected rows.

User.update({"active": False}, id=1)

docs.title: validate_schema(strict=False) classmethod
docs.text:
Compare model field definitions against the live DB schema.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default strict bool

if True raises SchemaError on mismatch

False

Returns:

Type Description dict

{ "table" : "users", "valid" : True | False, "missing_in_db" : ["phone"], "extra_in_db" : ["old_col"], "matched" : ["id", "username", ...]

dict

}

Usage

result = User.validate_schema() User.validate_schema(strict=True)

docs.title: mydborm.session.Session
docs.text:
Unit of Work + Identity Map session.

Tracks object lifecycle, detects dirty fields, and coordinates atomic flushes to the database. Use as context manager for automatic commit/rollback.

Usage

docs.title: As context manager
docs.text: with Session() as session: user = session.get(User, id=1) user.username = "updated" # auto-committed on exit

docs.title: Manual
docs.text: session = Session() session.configure(dialect="mysql", ...) user = session.get(User, id=1) user.username = "updated" session.flush() session.commit() session.close()

docs.title: add(model_class, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Queue a new object for insertion on next flush.

Usage

session.add(User, username="alice", email="a@x.com") session.add(Order, user_id=1, total=99.99) session.flush() # both inserted atomically

docs.title: all(model_class)
docs.text:
Load all records, registering each in the identity map.

Usage

users = session.all(User)

docs.title: close()
docs.text: Close the session and release all tracked objects.

docs.title: commit()
docs.text: Commit the current database transaction.

docs.title: delete(instance)
docs.text:
Mark an instance for deletion on next flush.

Usage

user = session.get(User, id=1) session.delete(user) session.flush()

docs.title: dirty_fields(instance)
docs.text: Return list of modified field names for an instance.

docs.title: expunge(instance)
docs.text: Remove an instance from the session without deleting it.

docs.title: expunge_all()
docs.text: Remove all instances from the session.

docs.title: filter(model_class, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Filter records, registering each in the identity map.

Usage

active_users = session.filter(User, active=True)

docs.title: flush()
docs.text:
Write all pending changes to the database without committing.

Order: INSERTs → UPDATEs → DELETEs

docs.title: get(model_class, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Get a record — returns cached instance if already loaded.

Usage

user = session.get(User, id=1) same = session.get(User, id=1) assert user is same # identical object

docs.title: is_dirty(instance)
docs.text: Check if an instance has unsaved changes.

docs.title: original_value(instance, field)
docs.text: Return the original DB value of a field before modification.

docs.title: rollback()
docs.text: Rollback pending changes — clear queued inserts/deletes and reset dirty tracking.

docs.title: stats()
docs.text: Return session statistics.

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
import asyncio
from mydborm.async_db import async_db, AsyncBaseModel
from mydborm.fields import IntField, StrField

class AsyncUser(AsyncBaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    id       = IntField(primary_key=True)
    username = StrField(max_length=100, nullable=False)

async def main():
    await async_db.configure(
        dialect  = "mysql",
        host     = "127.0.0.1",
        port     = 3306,
        user     = "root",
        password = "root",
        database = "mydb",
    )
    await AsyncUser.create_table()
    uid  = await AsyncUser.create(username="alice")
    user = await AsyncUser.get(id=uid)
    all  = await AsyncUser.all()
    await async_db.close()

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: YugabyteDB async
docs.text:
await async_db.configure(
    dialect  = "yugabyte",
    host     = "127.0.0.1",
    port     = 5433,
    user     = "yugabyte",
    password = "yugabyte",
    database = "yugabyte",
)

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
# Show version
mydborm version

# Test connectivity
mydborm ping --dialect mysql --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3306 --password root

# List tables
mydborm tables --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root

# Inspect schema
mydborm inspect --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root

# Migration status
mydborm migrate --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root --status

# Apply migration
mydborm migrate --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root \
                --model myapp.models.User

# Generate migration file
mydborm generate --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root \
                 --model myapp.models.User --output migrations/

# Connection pool status
mydborm pool --dialect mysql --database mydb --password root

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • MySQL 8+ or YugabyteDB 2.x+

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
# Core ORM
pip install mydborm

# With CLI support
pip install mydborm[cli]

# With async support
pip install mydborm[async]

# All extras
pip install mydborm[dev,cli,async]

docs.title: Docker quickstart
docs.text:
services:
  mysql:
    image: mysql:8.0
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: root
      MYSQL_DATABASE: mydb
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"

  yugabyte:
    image: yugabytedb/yugabyte:latest
    ports:
      - "5433:5433"

docker compose up -d

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
from mydborm import db

# MySQL
db.configure(
    dialect  = "mysql",
    host     = "127.0.0.1",
    port     = 3306,
    user     = "root",
    password = "yourpassword",
    database = "mydb",
    charset  = "utf8mb4",
)

# YugabyteDB
db.configure(
    dialect  = "yugabyte",
    host     = "127.0.0.1",
    port     = 5433,
    user     = "yugabyte",
    password = "yugabyte",
    database = "yugabyte",
)

# Via environment variable
import os
os.environ["DATABASE_URL"] = "mysql://root:password@localhost:3306/mydb"
db.from_env()

docs.title: Apply migration
docs.text:
from mydborm.migrations import migrate, migration_status, rollback

migrate(User, description="create users table")

docs.title: Check status
docs.text:
for m in migration_status():
    print(m["version"], m["description"], m["applied_at"])

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text: rollback(User)

docs.title: Auto-generate SQL files
docs.text:
from mydborm.migrations import generate, apply_migration_file

result = generate(User, output_dir="migrations/")
print(result["file"])  # migrations/0001_user.sql

apply_migration_file("migrations/0001_user.sql")

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
mydborm migrate --dialect mysql --status
mydborm generate --model myapp.models.User --output migrations/
mydborm generate --model myapp.models.User --apply

docs.title: Defining a model
docs.text:
from mydborm import BaseModel, IntField, StrField, BoolField, FloatField

class User(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    id       = IntField(primary_key=True)
    username = StrField(max_length=100, nullable=False)
    email    = StrField(max_length=255, nullable=False, unique=True)
    active   = BoolField(default=True)
    score    = FloatField(nullable=True)

docs.title: Field types
docs.text: Field MySQL YugabyteDB Python type IntField INT INTEGER int StrField VARCHAR(n) VARCHAR(n) str TextField TEXT TEXT str BoolField TINYINT(1) BOOLEAN bool FloatField FLOAT FLOAT float DecimalField DECIMAL(p,s) DECIMAL(p,s) Decimal DateField DATE DATE date DateTimeField DATETIME TIMESTAMP datetime JSONField JSON JSONB dict ForeignKeyField INT INTEGER int

docs.title: Validators
docs.text:
from mydborm import EmailValidator, RangeValidator, ChoiceValidator

class Profile(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "profiles"
    id    = IntField(primary_key=True)
    email = StrField(validators=[EmailValidator()])
    age   = IntField(validators=[RangeValidator(0, 150)])
    role  = StrField(validators=[ChoiceValidator(["admin", "user"])])

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
User.query().where("active", True).all()
User.query().where("age__gt", 18).all()
User.query().where("email__like", "%@example.com").all()
User.query().where("id__in", [1, 2, 3]).all()

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator SQL field = value field__gt > value field__lt < value field__gte >= value field__lte <= value field__ne != value field__like LIKE value field__in IN (...) field__null IS NULL

docs.title: Sorting, limit, offset
docs.text:
User.query().order_by("username").all()
User.query().order_by("score", desc=True).limit(10).offset(20).all()

docs.title: Aggregates
docs.text:
User.query().count()
User.query().where("active", True).sum("score")
User.query().avg("score")
User.query().min("score")
User.query().max("score")

docs.title: JOINs
docs.text:
User.query().inner_join("orders", "users.id = orders.user_id").all()
User.query().left_join("orders", "users.id = orders.user_id").all()

docs.title: GROUP BY + HAVING
docs.text: Order.query().group_by("user_id").having("COUNT(*) > 5").all()

docs.title: Subqueries
docs.text:
active_ids = User.query().where("active", True).subquery("id")
Order.query().where("user_id__in", active_ids).all()

docs.title: 1. Define a model
docs.text:
from mydborm import db, BaseModel, IntField, StrField, BoolField, FloatField

db.configure(dialect="mysql", host="127.0.0.1", port=3306,
             user="root", password="root", database="mydb")

class Product(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "products"
    id       = IntField(primary_key=True)
    name     = StrField(max_length=100, nullable=False)
    price    = FloatField(nullable=False)
    active   = BoolField(default=True)

docs.title: 2. Create table
docs.text: Product.create_table()

docs.title: 3. CRUD
docs.text:
# Create
pid = Product.create(name="Widget", price=9.99, active=True)

# Read
product  = Product.get(id=pid)
products = Product.all()
active   = Product.filter(active=True)

# Update
Product.update({"price": 12.99}, id=pid)

# Delete
Product.delete(id=pid)

docs.title: 4. Query builder
docs.text:
results = (Product.query()
                  .where("active", True)
                  .where("price__lt", 20.0)
                  .order_by("name")
                  .limit(10)
                  .all())

docs.title: 5. Migrations
docs.text:
from mydborm.migrations import migrate, generate

# Apply migration
migrate(Product, description="create products table")

# Generate SQL file
generate(Product, output_dir="migrations/")

docs.title: has_many
docs.text:
author = Author.get(id=1)
books  = author.has_many(Book, foreign_key="author_id")

docs.title: belongs_to
docs.text:
book   = Book.get(id=1)
author = book.belongs_to(Author, foreign_key="author_id")

docs.title: many_to_many
docs.text:
student = Student.get(id=1)
courses = student.many_to_many(
    Course,
    join_table = "student_courses",
    source_key = "student_id",
    target_key = "course_id"
)

docs.title: Lazy loading
docs.text:
from mydborm.model import LazyRelation

class Author(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = "authors"
    id    = IntField(primary_key=True)
    name  = StrField(max_length=100)
    books = LazyRelation("Book", foreign_key="author_id")

author = Author.get(id=1)
books  = author.books   # loaded on first access, cached after

docs.title: Eager loading
docs.text:
authors = Author.query().include("books").all()
for a in authors:
    print(a.books)  # already loaded, no extra queries

docs.title: Identity map
docs.text:
from mydborm import Session

session = Session()
u1 = session.get(User, id=1)
u2 = session.get(User, id=1)
assert u1 is u2  # same object

docs.title: Change tracking
docs.text:
u1["username"] = "updated"
session.is_dirty(u1)        # True
session.dirty_fields(u1)    # ["username"]
session.original_value(u1, "username")  # "alice"
session.flush()             # writes to DB

docs.title: Unit of work
docs.text:
session.add(User, username="new_user", email="x@x.com")
session.flush()  # INSERT

docs.title: Context manager
docs.text:
with Session() as session:
    u = session.get(User, id=1)
    u["username"] = "updated"
    # auto-committed on exit, rolled back on exception

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
u["username"] = "will_be_discarded"
session.rollback()
print(u["username"])  # original value restored

docs.title: Basic transaction
docs.text:
with db.transaction():
    db.execute("INSERT INTO users (username) VALUES (%s)", ["alice"])
    db.execute("INSERT INTO profiles (user_id) VALUES (%s)", [1])

docs.title: Savepoints
docs.text:
with db.transaction():
    User.create(username="alice")
    try:
        with db.savepoint("after_alice"):
            User.create(username="bob")
            raise Exception("bob failed")
    except Exception:
        pass  # only bob rolled back

docs.title: Bulk transaction
docs.text:
with db.bulk_transaction():
    db.execute("INSERT INTO orders ...")
    db.execute("INSERT INTO order_items ...")

docs.title: Nested transactions
docs.text:
with db.transaction():
    User.create(username="outer")
    with db.nested_transaction():
        User.create(username="inner")

docs.title: Retry on deadlock
docs.text:
with db.transaction_with_retry(retries=3):
    db.execute("UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 ...")
    db.execute("UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 ...")
